memoryArtifacts.headline: Historical Marker for Lawne's Creek Church, Virginia
memoryArtifacts.description: In 1624, Thomas Bennett Jr. and his wife, Agnes Beard Bennett, lived on the south side of the James River, near Lawne's Creek.  This is around the time that their children, John Bennett and Philip Bennett, were born.

zh.lifeSummary: Agnes Beard是William Beard的女兒，於1608年12月23日出生在英國薩默塞特維維利斯可姆。她於1623年7月17日與Thomas Bennett在英國英格蘭薩默塞特維維利斯可姆結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1660年9月26日在她的家鄉享年51歲。

nameMeanings.name: Beard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: nickname for a bearded man (from Middle English berd, Old English beard). To be clean-shaven was the norm in non-Jewish communities in northwestern Europe from the 12th to the 16th century, the crucial period for surname formation. There is a placename and other evidence to show that this word was used as a byname in the Old English period, when beards were the norm; in this period the byname would have referred to a large or noticeable beard. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates and equivalents in other languages, in particular German Barth 1.

habitational name from a place called Beard in Derbyshire (now represented by Beard Hall and Beardwood Farms in New Mills parish), which derives its name by dissimilation from Old English brerd ‘rim, bank’.

variants.name: Bardwell
meanings: English (Suffolk): habitational name from Bardwell in Suffolk, so named with an unattested Old English byname Bearda, a derivative of beard (see Beard ) + Old English well(a) ‘spring, stream’. Alternatively, the first element may be from a dissimilated form of Old English bre(o)rd ‘brim, bank’.

variants.name: Barth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Hans, Kurt, Franz, Erwin, Florian, Gerhard, Gunther, Klaus, Manfred, Armin, Arno.

German and Swiss German: nickname for a bearded man, from Middle High German bart ‘beard’. This name, which can not be always distinguished from 2 and 4 below, is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, and Slovenia, in the last four countries sometimes as a German translation of Slavic surnames meaning ‘beard’ or ‘bearded man’ (see Bart 1). See also Beard 1.
German: habitational name from a place so named in Pomerania.

variants.name: Bard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Michel, Aime, Alphonse, Alphy, Armand, Cecile, Celestine, Emile, Fernand, Germaine, Gilles, Jacques.

English (of Norman origin) and French: from the Old French personal name Bard(e), ancient Germanic Bardo, from barta ‘battle axe’. This was borne as a surname by a prominent Norman family with lands in west Normandy and in various English and Scottish counties, including Essex, Hertfordshire, North Yorkshire, Northumberland, and Lanarkshire. In Scotland the original family were Norman landowners in Strathaven parish in Lanarkshire. They were descended from the Baards, lords of Loftus in the North Yorkshire, descendants of the mid 12th-century Richard Baart.
Irish: altered form of either   Beard   or   Baird  .

variants.name: Bearb
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Aurelien, Camille, Micheline, Pierre.
Altered form of English Beard , under the influence, in LA, of French barbe ‘beard’.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: Agnes Beard was born on 23 December 1608, in Wiveliscombe, Somerset, England as the daughter of William Beard. She married Thomas Bennett on 17 July 1623, in Wiveliscombe, Somerset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 26 September 1660, in her hometown, at the age of 51.

fr.lifeSummary: Agnes Beard est née le 23 décembre 1608, à Wiveliscombe, Somerset, Angleterre, fille de William Beard. Elle a épousé Thomas Bennett le 17 juillet 1623, à Wiveliscombe, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 26 septembre 1660, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 51 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Agnes Beard wurde am 23. Dezember 1608 in Wiveliscombe, Somerset, England, als Tochter von William Beard geboren. Sie hat am 17. Juli 1623 in Wiveliscombe, Somerset, England, United Kingdom Thomas Bennett geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 26. September 1660 im Alter von 51 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Agnes Beard è nata il il 23 dicembre 1608 nel Wiveliscombe, Somerset, England come figlia di William Beard. Lei sposato Thomas Bennett il 17 luglio 1623, nel Wiveliscombe, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 26 settembre 1660, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 51 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Beardは1608年12月23日にイギリスソメルセトウィベリスコンベでWilliam Beardの娘として生まれた。1623年7月17日にイギリスイギリスソメルセトウィベリスコンベでThomas Bennettと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1660年9月26日に故郷で51歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Beard(은)는 1608년 12월 23일 영국 서머세트 위벨리스코브에서 William Beard의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1623년 7월 17일 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 위벨리스코브에서 Thomas Bennett(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1660년 9월 26일 그녀의 고향에서 51세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.23 December 1608–26 September 1660: 23 de dezembro de 1608–26 de setembro de 1660
pt.lifeSummary: Agnes Beard nasceu em 23 de dezembro de 1608, em Wiveliscombe, Somerset, Inglaterra filha de William Beard. Ela casou-se com Thomas Bennett em 17 de julho de 1623, em Wiveliscombe, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 26 de setembro de 1660, em sua cidade natal, com 51 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Agnes Beard родилась 23 декабря 1608 г. в местности вивелискомб, сомирсет, Англия. Её отца звали William Beard. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Bennett 17 июля 1623 г. в местности вивелискомб, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 26 сентября 1660 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 51 год.

Se casó con Thomas Bennett el 17 de julio de 1623, en Wiveliscombe, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 26 de septiembre de 1660, en su pueblo, a la edad de 51 años.
